summary: Pi: on the structured/json transport a mid-turn Deliver QUEUES (does not steer), the turn completes cleanly, and the queued message is provably delivered-or-retained, never lost
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.routing_expectation: structured is query-only: Deliver must return queued, not steered; the queue drains at the next LLM-call boundary (possibly within this turn)
output.turn_answer:
The command has finished executing. The word it printed is:

COMMAND_DONE

TEAL_c3fa6a15d4b8

agent_review.notes: Live proof of the transport-blind-steering fix on real Pi structured (--print --mode json). delivery_mode="queued" (pre-fix would have been "steered"). Pi's answer includes both the original tool result (COMMAND_DONE) and the queued word (TEAL_...), proving the sleep tool completed AND the mid-turn queued message reached the model at the next LLM-call boundary and was obeyed. Queue path delivers end-to-end on structured Pi. Third of three structured providers confirmed (Codex/Cursor/Pi all queued-not-steered); Claude is n/a — no structured lane.
